title: Fallingwater
description: Reinforced-concrete trays cantilevered over Bear Run — structure and site resolved as one system.
body: Commissioned by the Kaufmann family, the house is anchored to a natural rock ledge and cantilevers its living spaces directly over the falls of Bear Run. Reinforced-concrete trays step outward from a central sandstone core, each slab working as a stiffened beam-and-parapet section rather than a simple plate.

The cantilevers deflected from the day the formwork was struck — a well-documented case of under-reinforcement that culminated in a full post-tensioning intervention in 2002. It remains a canonical study in the gap between structural intent and structural behavior.
